entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (a) Describe the concept and scope of business studies, and its relationship with other disciplines
teaching_learning_activities: Scenario: In manageable groups provide a scenario showing various activities involved in the production, distribution and consumption of a product. Guide students to share what they have learnt from the scenario
Jigsaw: Guide students through jigsaw, to explore relationship of business studies and other subjects
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the importance of business studies in the community. Relate their responses to the importance of business studies
Resourceful person: Invite a guest to share with the students on the relationship between business studies and other disciplines. Then, guide the students to summarise what they have learnt
entries.assessment: The concept and scope of business studies is clearly described
resources: Online/offline sources
business club guidelines
video clips showing various business activities
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to identify various clubs that exist in schools. Then, relate their responses to the concept of a school business club, its meaning, importance and how it works. Provide students with the business club guidelines and milestones. Ask each group to work on one component of the simple constitution of a club such as membership, leadership, finance, disputes, and dissolution, and share. Project activity: Task students to compile all the components (developed in the skills lab) into one document. Guide students to make several amendments into a club constitution and use it to formulate the business club. Follow-up on project activity: Let students reflect on what mindsets and skills they had to use to create the constitution and how they might use those skills in the future as entrepreneurs or business owners
teaching_learning_activities: Skills lab: Organise students in manageable groups to identify various clubs that exist in schools. Then, relate their responses to the concept of a school business club, its meaning, importance and how it works. Provide students with the business club guidelines and milestones. Ask each group to work on one component of the simple constitution of a club such as membership, leadership, finance, disputes, and dissolution, and share

Project activity: Task students to compile all the components (developed in the skills lab) into one document. Guide students to make several amendments into a club constitution and use it to formulate the business club

Follow-up on project activity: Let students reflect on what mindsets and skills they had to use to create the constitution and how they might use those skills in the future as entrepreneurs or business owners
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (b) Describe the concept and theories of entrepreneurship (meaning, characteristics, skills and importance)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorm: Organise students in manageable groups to generate a list of different activities performed in their community. Then, discuss what it takes to do those activities and be successful. Relate the discussion to the meaning and characteristics of a good entrepreneur, based on their discussions

Field trip: Assign students in manageable groups to visit nearby businesses to explore the importance of their activities to the community and the skills they might need to become successful entrepreneurs. Let groups share their findings with the rest of the class through presentations

Role play/simulation: Assign students in manageable groups to prepare the simulation game on various theories and approaches to entrepreneurship by choosing a theme that is relevant to a particular theory (innovation theory, economic theory, sociological theory, opportunity and resource-based theory)
Let students discuss lessons learnt from the simulation. Relate their ideas with the theories of entrepreneurship
Then, synthesise what students have presented and make a conclusion on the intended lesson
entries.assessment: The concept and theories of entrepreneurship are well described
resources: Online/offline sources
video clips showing various business activities
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe sole proprietorship (meaning, features, advantages and disadvantages and start-up)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the businesses found in their surroundings and their owners. Guide them to explain why they think some businesses are owned by one person and others by more than one person. Relate their findings to the meaning and features of sole proprietorship legal form of business

Guest speaker: Invite a sole proprietor to the school to share with students on his or her experience on advantages and disadvantages of being a sole proprietor. Let students summarise what they have learnt
entries.assessment: Sole proprietorship is clearly described
resources: Online/offline sources from Business Registration and Lincesing Authority (BRELA)
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.learning_activity: (c) Describe sole proprietorship (meaning, features, advantages and disadvantages and start-up)
teaching_learning_activities: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the businesses found in their surroundings and their owners. Guide them to explain why they think some businesses are owned by one person and others by more than one person. Relate their findings to the meaning and features of sole proprietorship legal form of business

Guest speaker: Invite a sole proprietor to the school to share with students on his or her experience on advantages and disadvantages of being a sole proprietor. Let students summarise what they have learnt
entries.assessment: Sole proprietorship is clearly described
resources: Online/offline sources from Business Registration and Lincesing Authority (BRELA)
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education

entries.specific_competence: 1.1 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, theories and principles of Business Education
entries.learning_activity: Project activity: Task students in a group(s) to write a simple report/presentation on how to register a sole proprietorship in Tanzania. Case study: Provide case studies on sole proprietor business start-up processes of a real person (from capital acquisition, the business idea, resources needed, customers and market). Guide students in pairs to discuss and share some things they would consider when starting up their own businesses
teaching_learning_activities: Project activity: Task students in a group(s) to write a simple report/presentation on how to register a sole proprietorship in Tanzania
Case study: Provide case studies on sole proprietor business start-up processes of a real person (from capital acquisition, the business idea, resources needed, customers and market)
Guide students in pairs to discuss and share some things they would consider when starting up their own businesses
entries.references: TIE, Business Studies for Secondary Schools Form One: Student's Book and Teacher's Guide (2023 Edition), Tanzania Institute of Education
